message.content:
Review code quality for Task 3: Backend — Upload de Imagem e Prompt Engineering.

WHAT_WAS_IMPLEMENTED: Added constants block (_CRIATIVOS_MODOS, model dicts, system prompts, _replicate_headers) and 3 routes to jake_desktop/app.py: POST /api/criativos/upload-imagem, POST /api/criativos/expandir-prompt, POST /api/criativos/analisar-referencia.

PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS: Task 3 from /root/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-21-fabrica-de-criativos.md

BASE_SHA: 1b3f4a0f1021071bf99d5b9459f39d7218a3ee1b
HEAD_SHA: 0bce465 (run `git log --oneline -3` in /root to get full SHA)

DESCRIPTION: Flask backend routes for Fábrica de Criativos — image upload to Replicate, prompt expansion via Claude, image style analysis via Claude vision.

Focus on: error handling, security (no secrets in responses), correct use of existing helpers _get_db/_anthropic_client, JSON validation, mode enum validation.

content.text: I'll start by gathering all the context needed for this review in parallel.

1→# Fábrica de Criativos v2 — Implementation Plan
     2→
     3→> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
     4→
     5→**Goal:** Substituir a Fábrica de Criativos atual por versão completa com geração de imagem/vídeo (11 modelos Replicate), 5 especialistas de prompt via Claude, upload de referência, histórico paginado com pastas, e skill global do Claude Code.
     6→
     7→**Architecture:** Backend Flask com 13 novas rotas em `app.py`; geração de vídeo assíncrona com polling via `GET /status/<prediction_id>`; frontend Split View (config esquerda / resultado direita) em IIFE Vanilla JS; banco Neon com 2 novas tabelas; skill Markdown com 5 engenheiros de prompt especializados.
     8→
     9→**Tech Stack:** Python/Flask, psycopg2 (Neon PostgreSQL), Replicate REST API (sem SDK), Anthropic claude-sonnet-4-6, Vanilla JS (IIFE), CSS Glassmorphism (padrão Jake OS)
    10→
    11→**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-21-fabrica-de-criativos-design.md`
    12→
    13→---
    14→
    15→## File Map
    16→
    17→| Arquivo | Ação | Responsabilidade |
    18→|---|---|---|
    19→| `scripts/migrar_criativos.py` | Criar | Migration one-shot: tabelas + índices |
    20→| `jake_desktop/app.py` | Modificar | 13 novas rotas `/api/criativos/*` (upload-imagem, expandir-prompt, analisar-referencia, gerar-imagem, gerar-video, status/\<id\>, historico GET, historico POST, historico/\<id\> DELETE, historico/\<id\>/pasta PATCH, pastas GET, pastas POST, pastas/\<id\> DELETE) |
    21→| `jake_desktop/templates/dashboard.html` | Modificar | Substituir seção `page-criativos` (linhas 690–840) + link CSS/JS |
    22→| `jake_desktop/static/js/criativos.js` | Criar | IIFE completo: layout, modos, upload, geração, histórico |
    23→| `jake_desktop/static/css/criativos.css` | Criar | Estilos da seção |
    24→| `~/.claude/skills/fabrica-de-criativos/SKILL.md` | Criar | Skill global com 5 especialistas de prompt |
    25→
    26→---
    27→

    31→|---|---|---|
    32→| `flux-1.1-pro` | `black-forest-labs/flux-1.1-pro` | imagem |

    43→

    45→
    46→## Task 1: Claude Code Skill — 5 Especialistas de Prompt
    47→
    48→**Files:**
    49→- Create: `~/.claude/skills/fabrica-de-criativos/SKILL.md`
    50→
    51→- [ ] **Step 1: Criar diretório e arquivo da skill**
    52→
    53→```bash
    54→mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/fabrica-de-criativos
    55→```
    56→
    57→Criar `~/.claude/skills/fabrica-de-criativos/SKILL.md`:
    58→
    59→```markdown
    60→---
    61→name: fabrica-de-criativos
    62→description: Use when generating AI images or videos for ad creatives, needing prompt engineering for Replicate models, or working on the Jake OS Fábrica de Criativos feature. Provides 5 specialized prompt engineers (Anúncios, Criativo, Psicodélico, Pessoas, Cena) and Replicate model reference.
    63→---
    64→
    65→# Fábrica de Criativos — Prompt Engineering Skill
    66→
    67→## Overview
    68→
    69→5 engenheiros de prompt especializados para geração de criativos com IA. Cada especialista expande um prompt simples em português para um prompt técnico profissional em inglês, otimizado para o tipo de criativo.
    70→
    71→**Regra universal:** Sempre gerar o prompt expandido em **inglês**. Manter o prompt expandido entre 50–120 palavras. Incluir sempre: sujeito principal, iluminação, estilo fotográfico/artístico, câmera/lente (quando aplicável), mood/atmosfera.
    72→
    73→---
    74→
    75→## Os 5 Especialistas
    76→
    77→### 🎯 Anúncios
    78→**Quando usar:** Criativos para Meta Ads, Google Ads, produtos, serviços, clínicas, negócios locais.
    79→
    80→**Estrutura canônica:**
    81→`[produto/serviço] advertisement, [sujeito + ação], [iluminação: studio/rim/softbox], [câmera: Canon/Sony 85mm f/1.4], commercial photography style, [background: clean/white/gradient], [color grading: warm/cool/neutral], [emoção: trust/confident/welcoming], Meta Ads format`
    82→
    83→**Vocabulário:** studio lighting, rim light, commercial photography, shallow depth of field, trust-inspiring, clean background, product hero shot, brand colors
    84→
    85→**Evitar:** artsy, experimental, surreal, cluttered backgrounds, dark moody tones
    86→
    87→**Exemplo:**
    88→- Simples: "clínica odontológica sorriso"
    89→- Expandido: "Professional dental clinic advertisement, confident woman smiling with perfect white teeth, soft studio lighting with rim light, shallow depth of field, Canon EOS R5 85mm f/1.4, commercial photography style, clean white background, warm color grading, trust-inspiring composition, Meta Ads format"
    90→
    91→---
    92→
    93→### 🎨 Conteúdo Criativo
    94→**Quando usar:** Arte conceitual, posts de redes sociais com apelo estético, storytelling visual, marcas de moda/lifestyle.
    95→
    96→**Estrutura canônica:**
    97→`[conceito] conceptual art, [sujeito + contexto], [composição: bold/dynamic/layered], [estilo: editorial/National Geographic/fine art], [iluminação: golden hour/dramatic/cinematic], [mood: narrative/emotional/striking], [técnica: high contrast/color grading]`
    98→
    99→**Vocabulário:** conceptual art, editorial style, bold composition, visual narrative, dynamic lighting, color contrast, fine art photography, storytelling
   100→

   201→**Files:**
   202→- Create: `scripts/migrar_criativos.py`
   203→
   204→- [ ] **Step 1: Criar script de migração**
   205→
   206→```python
   207→#!/usr/bin/env python3
   208→"""
   209→Script one-shot: cria tabelas creative_folders e creative_history no Neon.
   210→Executar: PYTHONPATH=/root python3 scripts/migrar_criativos.py
   211→"""
   212→import sys
   213→sys.path.insert(0, '/root')
   214→from core.db import get_conn
   215→
   216→SQL = """
   217→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS creative_folders (
   218→    id        SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   219→    nome      VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   220→    criado_em TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
   221→);
   222→
   223→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS creative_history (
   224→    id               SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   225→    tipo             VARCHAR(10)  NOT NULL CHECK (tipo IN ('imagem','video')),
   226→    modo             VARCHAR(20)  NOT NULL,
   227→    modelo           VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL,
   228→    prompt_original  TEXT         NOT NULL,
   229→    prompt_expandido TEXT         NOT NULL,
   230→    url_resultado    TEXT         NOT NULL,
   231→    folder_id        INTEGER      REFERENCES creative_folders(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   232→    criado_em        TIMESTAMP    DEFAULT NOW()
   233→);
   234→
   235→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_creative_history_tipo    ON creative_history(tipo);
   236→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_creative_history_folder  ON creative_history(folder_id);
   237→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_creative_history_criado  ON creative_history(criado_em DESC);
   238→"""
   239→
   240→if __name__ == "__main__":
   241→    conn = get_conn()
   242→    try:
   243→        cur = conn.cursor()
   244→        cur.execute(SQL)
   245→        conn.commit()
   246→        print("✓ Tabelas creative_folders e creative_history criadas com sucesso.")
   247→    except Exception as e:
   248→        print(f"✕ Erro: {e}")
   249→        sys.exit(1)
   250→    finally:
   251→        conn.close()
   252→```
   253→
   254→- [ ] **Step 2: Executar migração**
   255→
   256→```bash
   257→PYTHONPATH=/root /root/venv/bin/python3 /root/scripts/migrar_criativos.py
   258→```
   259→
   260→Expected: `✓ Tabelas creative_folders e creative_history criadas com sucesso.`
   261→
   262→- [ ] **Step 3: Verificar tabelas**
   263→
   264→```bash
   265→PYTHONPATH=/root /root/venv/bin/python3 -c "
   266→from core.db import get_conn
   267→conn = get_conn()
   268→cur = conn.cursor()
   269→cur.execute(\"SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_name IN ('creative_folders','creative_history')\")
   270→print(cur.fetchall())
   271→conn.close()
   272→"
   273→```
   274→
   275→Expected: `[('creative_folders',), ('creative_history',)]`
   276→
   277→- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**
   278→
   279→```bash
   280→git add scripts/migrar_criativos.py
   281→git commit -m "feat: migration — tabelas creative_folders e creative_history"
   282→```
   283→
   284→---
   285→

   368→def _replicate_headers():
   369→    token = os.getenv("REPLICATE_API_TOKEN", "").strip()
   370→    if not token:

   374→
   375→- [ ] **Step 2: Adicionar rota de upload de imagem**
   376→
   377→Logo após as constantes acima:
   378→
   379→```python
   380→@app.route("/api/criativos/upload-imagem", methods=["POST"])
   381→@login_required
   382→def criativos_upload_imagem():
   383→    if "arquivo" not in request.files:
   384→        return jsonify({"error": "Campo 'arquivo' ausente"}), 400
   385→    arquivo = request.files["arquivo"]
   386→    file_bytes = arquivo.read()
   387→    mime = arquivo.content_type or "image/jpeg"
   388→    # base64 para análise via Claude
   389→    b64 = base64.b64encode(file_bytes).decode("utf-8")
   390→    # Upload para Replicate Files API para uso como URL em I2V
   391→    try:
   392→        headers = _replicate_headers()

   408→```

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Jake OS – Plataforma SaaS completa.
Acesse via: http://localhost:5050
"""

import os
import io
import json
import base64
import uuid
import webbrowser
import threading
import time
import secrets as _secrets
from datetime import datetime
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo
from functools import wraps

from flask import Flask, render_template, jsonify, request, session, redirect, url_for

import requests
from dotenv import load_dotenv
import psycopg2
import psycopg2.extras

# Carrega .env do projeto ou do diretório pai (ex.: /root/.env)
_basedir = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
load_dotenv(os.path.join(_basedir, ".env"))
load_dotenv(os.path.join(os.path.dirname(_basedir), ".env"))

from openai import OpenAI
import anthropic as _anthropic

import sys as _sys
_sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
import meta.meta_api as _meta_api

_VALID_TOKEN_KEYS = {"META_TOKEN_PILOTI", "META_TOKEN_DENTTO", "META_ACCESS_TOKEN"}


def _get_db():
    """Abre conexão com Neon usando DATABASE_URL do .env."""
    db_url = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL", "").strip()
    if not db_url:
        raise RuntimeError("DATABASE_URL não definido no .env")
    return psycopg2.connect(db_url, cursor_factory=psycopg2.extras.RealDictCursor)

app = Flask(__name__, static_folder="static", template_folder="templates")
app.secret_key = os.environ.get("SESSION_SECRET") or _secrets.token_hex(32)

input.description: Check for MIME type validation in upload routes

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

content.text:
I now have everything needed for a thorough review. Let me compile the findings.

---

## Code Review — Task 3: Backend Upload de Imagem e Prompt Engineering

### What Was Done Well

The implementation is a faithful, near-exact copy of the plan spec. All three routes are present, all use `@login_required`, the helpers `_anthropic_client()` and `_replicate_headers()` are used correctly, `_get_db` is not touched (no DB calls are needed in this task, as specified), mode enum validation is in place on the `expandir-prompt` route, and `modo_sugerido` from Claude is sanitised before returning. The JSON parsing of Claude's response in `analisar-referencia` handles the markdown fence case. Error messages do not expose API tokens or internal stack traces.

---

### Issues Found

#### Critical

**1. `import math as _math` and `import time as _t` placed in module body at line ~1951 — duplicate and misplaced imports.**

`math` is never used anywhere in the added block. `time` is already imported at line 14 as `time` and again at line 1018 inside a function as `import time as _time`. Adding a third import alias in module scope is harmless at runtime but signals that the imports were copied from the plan spec without being verified against what already exists. `_math` especially is dead code — if it is meant for future tasks (e.g. ceiling pagination), it should be added at the top of the file with the other standard-library imports, not in the middle of the module.

File: `/root/jake_desktop/app.py`, line 1951–1952.

**2. `upload-imagem` sends raw `base64` of the uploaded file back to the client in the response body.**

```python
return jsonify({"url": url, "base64": b64, "mime_type": mime, "ok": True})
```

A large image (e.g. 5 MB) becomes a ~6.7 MB base64 string that is serialised into the JSON response over the loopback connection. The JavaScript then has to hold this string in memory for later use in `analisar-referencia`. This is a design choice inherited from the plan, but the implementation adds no size guard before `arquivo.read()`. If a user uploads a 50 MB file, the server reads the whole thing into a Python `bytes` object, base64-encodes it, AND sends it to Replicate — all in a single blocking request with a 30-second timeout. This will exhaust server memory on the VPS.

The plan itself did not specify a size limit; this is a gap in the plan that the implementation should have caught and added defensively.

#### Important

**3. `analisar-referencia` accepts an arbitrary `mime_type` string from the client and passes it directly to the Anthropic API without validation.**

```python
mime = d.get("mime_type", "image/jpeg")
# ... passed directly to:
{"type": "image", "source": {"type": "base64", "media_type": mime, "data": b64}}
```

Anthropic's vision API accepts only `image/jpeg`, `image/png`, `image/gif`, `image/webp`. Sending an unsupported type (e.g. `image/bmp`, or a crafted string) will cause an Anthropic API error that propagates back via the generic `except Exception as e: return jsonify({"error": str(e)}), 500`. The error message from the Anthropic SDK can be verbose and may expose internal detail. A whitelist check before the API call is the correct fix.

**4. `upload-imagem` trusts `arquivo.content_type` (client-supplied) as the MIME type sent to Replicate.**

`content_type` on a Flask `FileStorage` object comes from the multipart `Content-Type` header, which the browser sets but which can be spoofed. There is no file-extension or magic-byte check. An attacker authenticated to the app could upload a non-image file with `Content-Type: image/jpeg`. The existing `anuncios_upload_criativo` route in the same file also does not validate this, so the pattern is consistent, but that does not make it correct.

**5. `_CRIATIVOS_MODELOS_VIDEO` is defined at module scope but is not used by any route in this task.**

The plan says Task 3 is upload + prompt only; video generation is Task 5. Defining the video model dict here is fine and matches the plan spec. However, `_CRIATIVOS_MODELOS_IMAGEM` is also not used in Task 3 — both dicts are forward declarations for Tasks 4 and 5. This is intentional per the plan, but it means two constants sit in the codebase referencing model slugs that may have changed between the time the plan was written and when Task 4 is implemented. This is worth noting as a maintenance risk.

**6. The `headers.pop("Content-Type")` pattern is fragile.**

```python
headers = _replicate_headers()
headers.pop("Content-Type")  # multipart não usa Content-Type JSON
resp = requests.post(
    ...,
    headers={"Authorization": headers["Authorization"]},
    ...
)
```

The code calls `_replicate_headers()` to get both keys, immediately discards `Content-Type` via `.pop()`, then builds a new dict with only `Authorization`. This is correct behaviour but the intermediate `.pop()` call is misleading — it mutates a local dict that is then thrown away. The comment says why, which helps, but the correct and self-documenting pattern used elsewhere (e.g. the existing `_generate_flux` function) is to simply build the header dict directly without calling `_replicate_headers()` at all, or to pass only `Authorization` from the start. If `_replicate_headers()` ever adds more required headers, this route will silently drop them.

#### Suggestions

**7. No `tipo` field validation in `expandir-prompt`.**

`tipo` defaults to `"imagem"` and the only branch checks `if tipo == "video"`. Any other value (e.g. `"foto"`, `""`) silently falls through to the image path. This is fine behaviourally because the fallback is safe, but an explicit validation or normalisation (accepting only `"imagem"` / `"video"`) would make the contract clearer.

**8. The markdown-fence stripping in `analisar-referencia` is brittle.**

```python
if "```" in raw:
    raw = raw.split("```")[1]
    if raw.startswith("json\n"):
        raw = raw[5:]
```

This same pattern is used identically in `anuncios_gerar_copy` at line ~1820 of the existing code, so it is consistent. However, `.split("```")[1]` will fail silently if Claude wraps the response in a fence with no language tag (e.g. ` ``` ` alone), because `[1]` would then contain the content correctly but `raw.startswith("json\n")` would be false and the raw string would still start with `\n`. The result would be a `json.JSONDecodeError`, which is caught. This is acceptable for an internal tool.

**9. `_REPLICATE_BASE` is defined here at line 2011 but the existing `_generate_flux` function at line 1023 hardcodes the full URL directly.**

There is now an inconsistency: new code uses `_REPLICATE_BASE` constant, old code does not. Not a bug, but if the base URL ever changes (e.g. an API version bump), two places would need updating.

---

### Plan Alignment

The implementation matches the plan spec exactly — same code, same structure, same commit message. All four plan steps (constants, upload route, expand-prompt route, analyse-reference route) are present. No deviations from the plan were found.

The issues identified above are either gaps in the plan itself (no file size limit specified — items 2, 4) or pre-existing patterns in the codebase that were faithfully reproduced (item 8). The one item that should have been caught against the plan is the duplicate/dead imports (item 1).

---

### Summary Table

| # | Severity | Issue | File / Line |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Critical | `import math as _math` is dead code; `import time as _t` duplicates existing top-level import | `app.py` line 1951–1952 |
| 2 | Critical | No file size limit before `arquivo.read()` — OOM risk on large uploads | `app.py` line 2027 |
| 3 | Important | `mime_type` from client not validated against Anthropic's accepted types in `analisar-referencia` | `app.py` line 2084 |
| 4 | Important | `arquivo.content_type` (client-supplied) trusted as MIME with no file-type verification | `app.py` line 2028 |
| 5 | Important | `headers.pop("Content-Type")` pattern is misleading and fragile | `app.py` line 2033–2040 |
| 6 | Suggestion | `tipo` field in `expandir-prompt` has no validation, silently accepts any string | `app.py` line 2057 |
| 7 | Suggestion | Markdown-fence stripping is brittle for responses without language tag | `app.py` line 2103–2106 |
| 8 | Suggestion | `_REPLICATE_BASE` constant not used by older `_generate_flux` — inconsistency | `app.py` lines 1023 vs 2011 |
